final_answer:
No. It is a GiltConsensus code-path issue, not just a one-off mistake from my side.

Why it matters: `tx stake ...` currently signs with `helper.GetPrivKey()` (from `priv_validator_key.json`) instead of the `--from` keyring account, so real stake transactions can fail with `invalid pubkey` even when the operator does everything correctly.

What should be done next:
1. Fix stake/common broadcast signing to use the `--from` keyring signer.
2. Rebuild `giltconsd`.
3. Re-run the same real-node test and confirm stake tx reaches the actual operator-uniqueness logic.
prompt: So its a signing issue from your side not a gilt chain code issue?
